recommendations: Add and run a minimal MCP-focused test suite (unit + smoke tests for the `transform` endpoint) in CI
Fix dependency detection/parsing (analysis missed `pyproject.toml`/`setup.py`) and align packaging metadata validation checks
Define strict Pydantic request/response schemas for `transform` (method
direction
centering/options) with clear validation errors
Add input guards for numerical edge cases (NaN/inf
non-square arrays
dtype/shape constraints
invalid method params) and return actionable error messages
Introduce performance regression benchmarks for key algorithms (`basex`
`rbasex`
`hansenlaw`
`direct`) with size-based thresholds
Enable matrix CI across Python/NumPy/SciPy versions and OSes and publish a concise test summary artifact
Add golden-data consistency tests comparing MCP endpoint outputs to direct `abel.Transform` results across methods
Improve observability in MCP service (structured logs
timing
method selected
failure category) and add debug mode
Document endpoint contract and examples in `README_MCP.md` (common workflows
parameter presets
troubleshooting)
Add caching strategy controls for basis generation (warmup/precompute
cache location
invalidation) and test cache hit behavior
Create a small compatibility layer for optional deps (`matplotlib`) so runtime errors are deferred and informative
Add type checking and linting gates (mypy/ruff or flake8) for `mcp_output` plus basic docstring coverage
Implement payload size/rate limits and timeout controls in MCP service to prevent oversized transform requests
Add reproducible benchmark scripts and store baseline metrics in repo/CI artifacts for trend tracking
Add an end-to-end example client script that calls MCP `transform` and validates output shape/metadata

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status `success` and completed all planned nodes (`download`, `analysis`, `env`, `generate`, `run`, `review`, `finalize`).
Robust fallback behavior: clone failure was mitigated via zip fallback (`processed_by: zip_fallback`, 127 files discovered), allowing downstream analysis and generation to proceed.
MCP plugin boot/health checks passed (`service_health: healthy`, `transport_mode: stdio`).
Low intrusiveness import-adapter strategy (confidence ~0.9, import feasibility ~0.92) aligned well with PyAbel’s modular API.
failure_reasons: Primary repository download path failed due to network/connectivity issue (`curl 28`, github.com:443 timeout).
Dependency/structure detectors produced false negatives (`pyproject.toml` and `setup.py` exist but were reported as absent), reducing analysis fidelity.
Quality telemetry is incomplete (`llm_calls`, `total_files_processed`, generated LOC/size, perf metrics reported as zero), limiting confidence in quantitative scoring.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external contextual enrichment.

node_performance.download_time: Download node experienced retry/fallback overhead due to clone timeout (~75s on failed git path); resilience was good because zip fallback recovered execution.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed functionally (core modules and repo tree identified), but metadata extraction quality was inconsistent (dependency parser mismatch).
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed successfully and produced expected MCP scaffold files and endpoint wiring; likely efficient but exact node timing is unavailable.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin runtime health test passed quickly; original project tests were not executed meaningfully (reported failed with empty details), so validation depth is low.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB likely placeholder). No evidence of memory pressure in this run.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0% likely placeholder). No CPU bottleneck evidence captured.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate inferred usage (repo includes ~127 files plus generated plugin scaffold). Generated size metrics are missing/zero, so actual footprint is underreported.
